Context Example
There is no context output for this command.
Human Readable Output
13. Get a list of metric filters
Lists the specified metric filters. You can list all the metric filters or filter the results by log name, prefix, metric name, or metric namespace.
Base Command
aws-logs-describe-metric-filters

Command Example

name: aws-logs-describe-metric-filters

outputs.description: The name of the metric filter. AWS.CloudWatchLogs.MetricFilters.filterPattern A symbolic description of how CloudWatch Logs should interpret the data in each log event. For example, a log event may contain time stamps, IP addresses, strings, and so on. You use the filter pattern to specify what to look for in the log event message.
outputs.name: AWS.CloudWatchLogs.MetricFilters.filterName

outputs.description: The name of the CloudWatch metric. AWS.CloudWatchLogs.MetricFilters.metricTransformations.metricNamespace The namespace of the CloudWatch metric.
outputs.name: AWS.CloudWatchLogs.MetricFilters.metricTransformations.metricName

outputs.description: The value to publish to the CloudWatch metric when a filter pattern matches a log event. AWS.CloudWatchLogs.MetricFilters.metricTransformations.defaultValue The value to emit when a filter pattern does not match a log event. This value can be null.
outputs.name: AWS.CloudWatchLogs.MetricFilters.metricTransformations.metricValue

outputs.description: date The creation time of the metric filter, expressed as the number of milliseconds after Jan 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC. AWS.CloudWatchLogs.MetricFilters.logGroupName
outputs.name: AWS.CloudWatchLogs.MetricFilters.creationTime

outputs.description: The name of the log group.
outputs.name: string
